varieties.name: 1. Australian-Grown Organic (Queensland Darling Downs)
varieties.traits: Locally adapted ecotype, grown on rich basalt soils. Seeds are golden-brown, 3-4mm length
varieties.availability: Sovereign Foods, Eden Seeds, local Queensland organic suppliers

varieties.name: 2. Common Fenugreek (Standard Type)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean/West Asia origin, cultivated globally 6000+ years
varieties.traits: Standard fenugreek characteristics: 30-60cm height, trifoliate leaves, pale yellow flowers
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Eden Seeds, Australian seed suppliers

varieties.name: 3. Sprouting Fenugreek
varieties.traits: Fast-germinating seeds, suitable for microgreens
varieties.yield_notes: Ready to harvest as sprouts in 2-6 days
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection, Australian Wheatgrass Company, health food suppliers

varieties.name: 4. Kasuri Methi Type
varieties.origin: Kasur region Punjab (now Pakistan), traditional wild ecotype
varieties.traits: Smaller seed size than common fenugreek, slower growing, remains rosette during vegetative growth
varieties.yield_notes: Primarily grown for leaves - 5-8 leaf cuttings possible. Seeds 150-160 days
varieties.availability: Specialty Indian spice suppliers, online seed banks

varieties.name: 5. Green Manure/Cover Crop Strain
varieties.origin: Selected for biomass and nitrogen-fixing capacity
varieties.traits: Fast-growing, vigorous vegetative growth, deep tap root system
varieties.yield_notes: High biomass production, excellent nitrogen fixation
varieties.availability: Green Harvest, organic farm suppliers, permaculture suppliers

varieties.name: 1. Common Methi (Traditional Kerala Type)
varieties.origin: Mediterranean origin, naturalized in India 3000+ years, grown in Kerala for centuries
varieties.traits: Standard fenugreek - 30-60cm tall, pale green trifoliate leaves, cream-white flowers, 10-15cm pods
varieties.yield_notes: 10-11 quintals/ha average, up to 15-20 q/ha with good management
varieties.availability: Kerala Spices Online, local Kerala agricultural markets, farmers markets in Thiruvalla

varieties.name: 2. CO-1
varieties.traits: Improved variety for South India. Early maturing, uniform plant height, more branches
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, Kerala agricultural department, regional seed suppliers

varieties.name: 3. CO-2
varieties.traits: Improved selection from CO-1, enhanced characteristics for South India
varieties.availability: Kerala/Tamil Nadu agricultural universities, authorized seed dealers

varieties.name: 4. RMt-1 (Rajasthan Methi-1)
varieties.traits: Developed for lower rainfall areas but adaptable. Maximum pod per plant (98 in trials)
varieties.availability: National seed corporations, Kerala agricultural department

varieties.name: 5. RMt-143
varieties.traits: Maximum branching (8.33 branches/plant in trials), bushy growth habit

varieties.name: 6. RMt-303
varieties.traits: Tall variety (91cm height), maximum branching (8.33), long pods (11.33cm), 18 seeds per pod

varieties.name: 7. RMt-305
varieties.traits: Dwarf variety, quick-maturing pods, 18 seeds per pod, high test weight (14.3g)
varieties.availability: Regional agricultural suppliers, seed dealers

varieties.name: 8. RMt-351
varieties.traits: Improved variety, 17 seeds per pod, good branching
varieties.availability: Seed dealers, agricultural department

varieties.name: 9. Rajendra Kranti (Rajendra Kanti)
varieties.traits: Improved variety for eastern regions, maximum branching (8.33), 15 seeds per pod

varieties.name: 10. Pusa Early Bunching
varieties.traits: Early maturing, developed for leaf production, heavy branching
varieties.availability: IARI Pusa, national seed distributors, Kerala suppliers

varieties.name: 11. Pusa Kasuri
varieties.origin: IARI Pusa, developed from Kasuri methi types
varieties.traits: Kasuri methi characteristics - slower growing, smaller seeds, highly scented
varieties.yield_notes: Primarily for leaf production, 150-160 days to seed maturity
varieties.availability: IARI, specialized spice suppliers, online

varieties.name: 12. Hisar Sonali
varieties.traits: Golden-colored seeds ("Sonali" = golden), uniform plant characteristics
varieties.availability: Haryana agricultural university, pan-India seed distributors

varieties.name: 13. Hisar Suvarna
varieties.traits: Tall variety (87.67cm), maximum branching (8.33), "Suvarna" means golden

varieties.name: 14. Hisar Mukta
varieties.traits: Improved Hisar variety, good plant vigor

varieties.name: 15. Hisar Madhavi
varieties.traits: Improved selection from Hisar series, good branching
varieties.availability: Regional seed suppliers

varieties.name: 16. Pant Ragini
varieties.traits: Unique genetic characteristics, 78.66cm height, 17 seeds per pod, shortest pod (8.66cm)

varieties.name: 17. Lam Selection-1
varieties.traits: Selected for superior characteristics, maximum pod length (13.33cm in local trials)
varieties.yield_notes: 13-16 quintals/ha (notably 15 q/ha in local variety trials)
varieties.availability: Regional agricultural departments

varieties.name: 18. Gujarat Methi (GM-1)
varieties.traits: Developed for Gujarat conditions, moderate branching (7-8)

varieties.name: 19. Gujarat Methi (GM-2)
varieties.origin: Gujarat agricultural research, improvement on GM-1
varieties.traits: Tall variety (91cm), moderate branching (7-8)
varieties.availability: Major seed distributors

varieties.name: 20. Azad Methi (AM-1)
varieties.traits: Selected variety, moderate characteristics

varieties.name: 21. Azad Methi (AM-2)
varieties.traits: Improved characteristics over AM-1
varieties.availability: Seed corporations

varieties.name: 22. AFg-1
varieties.traits: Tall variety (97.33cm), heavy branching (8.00), many pods (56)
varieties.yield_notes: High performing - 14 q/ha (second highest in trials)
varieties.availability: ICAR-NBPGR, research institutions

varieties.name: 23. AFg-2
varieties.traits: Shortest variety (78.33cm), minimum branching (6.33), long pods (13.00cm)

varieties.name: 24. AFg-3
varieties.traits: Long pods (13.00cm), moderate height, high test weight (13.70g)

varieties.name: 25. Kasuri Methi (Trigonella corniculata)
varieties.origin: Kasur region Punjab (wild origin), traditional Indian cultivation
varieties.traits: SEPARATE SPECIES from common fenugreek. Smaller seeds, slower growth, rosette habit, bright orange-yellow flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Primarily leaf crop: 5-8 cuttings. Seeds: 150-160 days, 9-10 q/ha
varieties.availability: Specialty spice seed suppliers, Indian seed banks, Kerala spice dealers

varieties.name: 26. Local Kerala Ecotypes
varieties.origin: Traditional Kerala farmer selections, localized adaptation over generations
varieties.traits: Naturally adapted to Kerala monsoon conditions through generations of local selection
varieties.yield_notes: Variable 10-15 q/ha depending on local selection
varieties.availability: Local Kerala farmers markets, Thiruvalla agricultural markets, farmer seed exchanges, Kerala traditional seed networks
